zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Vaux於大約1261年出生在英國林可爾史爾福萊斯頓時，她的父親John de Vaux42歲，母親Sybil de Longchamps41歲。她於1287年與Sir William de Ros II在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1316年8月16日在英國諾福爾可特尼享年56歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可特尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Vaux于大约1261年出生在英国林可爾史爾福萊斯頓时，她的父亲John de Vaux42岁，母亲Sybil de Longchamps41岁。她于1287年与Sir William de Ros II在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1316年8月16日在英国諾福爾可特尼享年56岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可特尼。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vaux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of various places in northern France called Vaux, from the Old French plural of val ‘valley’. Compare English Vause and French Devaux .

History: Richard Vaux (1749–90) emigrated from Sussex, England, to Philadelphia, PA, in early youth. Calvert Vaux, landscape architect, emigrated from London, England, to America in 1850 and designed several major US parks, including Central Park in New York City (with Frederick Olmstead). — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its variant Veaux and altered form Devoe ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Vause
meanings: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Vaux (see Vaux ), a common French placename, singular and plural of val ‘valley’, the plural form being Latinized in surnames as de Vallibus. Norman lords with this surname are recorded in 12th- and early 13th-century records in Cumberland (Irthington), Devon (Farwood, in Colyton), East Yorkshire (Warter), Essex (Belchamp), and Leicestershire (Belvoir). Compare Vale .

nickname perhaps from Middle English fals, faus, vaus (Old French fals, faus, Old English fals) ‘deceitful, disloyal, dishonest, mendacious’, but it is more likely the name arises from 1, with the common Anglo-Norman French substitution of le for de in their surname.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Vaux was born about 1261, in Freiston, Lincolnshire, England, her father, John de Vaux, was 42 and her mother, Sybil de Longchamps, was 41. She married Sir William de Ros II in 1287, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died about 16 August 1316, in Pentney, Norfolk, England, at the age of 56, and was buried in Pentney, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Vaux est née environ 1261, à Freiston, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Vaux, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Sybil de Longchamps, avait 41 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de Ros II en 1287, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée environ 16 août 1316, à Pentney, Norfolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterrée à Pentney, Norfolk, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1287 in Yorkshire, England Sir William de Ros II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb um 16. August 1316 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Pentney, Norfolk, England und wurde in Pentney, Norfolk, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Vauxが約1261年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアフレイストンで生まれた時，父John de Vauxは42歳，母Sybil de Longchampsは41歳であった。1287年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでSir William de Ros IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1316年8月16日にイギリスノーフォルクペントニーで56歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクペントニーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Vaux(이)가 대략 1261년 영국 링코실르 프레이스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Vaux(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybil de Longchamps(은)는 41세였다. 그녀는 1287년 영국 요르크실르에서 Sir William de Ros II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1316년 8월 16일 영국 노르폴크 펜트니에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 펜트니에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir William de Ros II em 1287, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Vaux родилась около 1261 г. в местности фристон, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Vaux было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Sybil de Longchamps, 41. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de Ros II в 1287 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла около 16 августа 1316 г. в местности пентни, норфолк, Англия в возрасте 56 лет и была похоронена в местности пентни, норфолк, Англия.
